setting.note: Zafón's Barcelona of the postwar 1940s: a fog-bound old town of narrow lanes and antiquarian bookshops, from a hidden library in the Raval and a family shop off La Rambla out to the ruined modernista mansions above the city and the cemetery on Montjuïc.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Carlos Ruiz Zafón, first published 2001. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Shadow of the Wind, Carlos Ruiz Zafón (2001). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The misty blue of Zafón's night-time Barcelona, the colour of fog on the harbour and the lamplit streets of the old town. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
